summary: A medieval-style fortified castle structure with a rectangular footprint, featuring two prominent corner towers with crenellated tops, thick stonebrick walls, and a central courtyard. The building is surrounded by a decorative stone block pathway and sits on a dirt foundation. Interior layout shows symmetrical design with two side chambers and a central cross-shaped passage.

shape.overall_form: rectangular fortification with two corner towers

rebuild_hints: Build rectangular outer wall frame approximately 14×18 blocks using stonebrick
Construct two towers at opposite corners, each approximately 3×3 blocks wide and 8 blocks tall
Add crenellations (alternating slab pattern) along all wall tops and tower tops
Create interior cross-shaped passage with dark wood or brick flooring running north-south and east-west
Place two symmetrical side chambers (approximately 4×4 blocks each) on left and right of central passage
Add decorative stone block pathway around perimeter foundation
Install torch lighting at strategic interior and exterior points
Top towers with wooden roof caps and dark wood overhangs
Maintain 2-block thick walls throughout for fortress appearance
uncertainties: Exact interior room dimensions and wall thickness not fully visible from all angles
Specific window or door placements on interior walls unclear from overhead views
Exact height of towers relative to main walls estimated from perspective
Material composition of interior flooring (appears to be dirt/wood mix)
Whether structure has basement or underground levels not visible
